items.title: Deutsche Bank cuts Q3 gold price target by 22%
items.snippet: Deutsche Bank has become the latest major institution to revise down its near-term target for gold prices amid concerns over inflation and high interest rates. In a note published Tuesday, the German investment bank said it now sees gold averaging $4,300/oz. in the third quarter of this year, which is 22% lower than its previous forecast. items.snippet: The euro has sunk to a one-year low, with easing of tensions between the United States and Iran helping to cool oil prices and raise expectations that the European Central Bank will turn more dovish. The euro was quoted at $1.135 against the U.S. dollar on Wednesday, down from $1.165 before the U.S. and Iran first agreed to a conditional ceasefire on 8th April. items.title: China’s Teapot Refineries Cut Operations to Their Lowest Level Since 2017
items.snippet: The independent refiners in China, the so-called teapots, have slashed their refinery run rates to the lowest level since 2017, as high feedstock prices, weak domestic fuel consumption, and restricted exports have hit margins. items.title: India Cuts Coal Imports as Power Plants Turn to Domestic Fuel
items.snippet: India is boosting the share of domestic coal used in power plants designated to run on imported fuel, as rising local coal production and expanding renewables have helped to cut coal imports to multi-year lows.
